title: ESG Reporting Platform Selection Guide: Workiva vs Diligent vs Nasdaq ESG Small Business Comparison Review | 2026 Review
description: In-depth comparison of Workiva, Diligent, and Nasdaq ESG ESG reporting platforms. Analyzing data collection, report templates, and audit support capabiliti
content:
ESG Reporting Platform Background

In 2026, ESG information disclosure has become an essential capability for enterprise financing, listing, and brand building. Globally over 80% of institutional investors incorporate ESG performance into investment decisions, and domestic regulatory departments are also advancing mandatory ESG disclosure systems. SMEs face three major pain points in ESG reporting: scattered data difficult to collect, unfamiliar report standards, high preparation costs. Professional ESG reporting platform can automatically collect environmental, social, governance data, built-in GRI, SASB, ISSB etc. mainstream report framework templates, generate audit-compliant ESG reports, helping enterprises reduce preparation costs by over 50%. This article will review three mainstream ESG reporting platforms to provide decision basis for SME selection.

Core Feature Comparison Table

Feature DimensionWorkivaDiligentNasdaq ESGSME Suitability
Data CollectionAPI integration + Excel importManual entry mainlyAPI integration + questionnaire collectionWorkiva efficiency highest
Report FrameworkGRI + SASB + ISSB + customGRI + SASBGRI + ISSBWorkiva coverage most complete
Report Templates50+ industry templates20+ templates10+ templatesWorkiva selection most
Audit SupportInternal control process + version trackingBasic version recordNo built-in auditWorkiva compliance strongest
Visualization ChartsInteractive charts + exportStatic chartsInteractive dashboardNasdaq display optimal
Investor PortalNo built-inNo built-inBuilt-in investor displayNasdaq financing support strong

Workiva ESG Reporting Platform Detailed Explanation

Workiva is a global leading enterprise reporting platform, ESG reporting is its core module, emphasizing data automated collection and audit compliance.

  • Core Capabilities: API integration enterprise ERP, HR, environmental management systems, automatically capture ESG data; built-in GRI, SASB, ISSB, CDP etc. mainstream report frameworks; 50+ industry report templates, one-click generate standardized reports; version tracking, approval process, internal control evidence retention, meeting audit requirements.
  • Technical Advantages: XBRL format output, supports regulatory submission; multi-person collaborative editing, ESG team division collaboration; real-time data update, report synchronized with operational data; cloud architecture, no local deployment needed.
  • Disadvantage Analysis: Price relatively high, SME subscription burden larger; system learning curve steep, needs professional training; Chinese report templates few, domestic enterprises need customization.
  • Price System: ESG module annual fee $15,000-30,000; complete enterprise reporting platform annual fee $50,000+; SME ESG specialized version annual fee $10,000.

Diligent ESG Reporting Platform Detailed Explanation

Diligent is a board governance platform provider, ESG reporting module integrated into its governance solution, emphasizing board oversight and data security.

  • Core Capabilities: Manual data entry mainly, supports Excel batch import; built-in GRI, SASB report frameworks; 20+ industry report templates; basic version record function, meeting audit traceability requirements.
  • Technical Advantages: Board portal integrated, ESG report direct to board approval; data security level high, meeting financial institution compliance requirements; deployment cycle short, 2-4 weeks online; user interface simple, learning cost low.
  • Disadvantage Analysis: Data collection automation level low, relies on manual entry; report framework coverage few, lacking ISSB template; visualization effect basic, lacking interactive charts.
  • Price System: ESG module annual fee $8,000-15,000; complete governance platform annual fee $25,000+; suitable for enterprises already using Diligent governance platform.

Nasdaq ESG Reporting Platform Detailed Explanation

Nasdaq ESG is Nasdaq's ESG data management and investor display platform, emphasizing financing support and data visualization.

  • Core Capabilities: API integration enterprise systems + questionnaire collection supply chain data; built-in GRI, ISSB report frameworks; investor portal, ESG data direct to capital market; interactive visualization dashboard, enhancing data display effect.
  • Technical Advantages: Investor display function leading, ESG data push to Nasdaq platform; supply chain questionnaire collection, covering ESG full chain data; interactive charts, investors can customize viewing dimensions; Chinese interface support, suitable for domestic enterprises.
  • Disadvantage Analysis: Report templates few, only 10+ industry templates; lacking audit support function, internal control process needs external; only targeting listed companies or pre-listed enterprises, SME applicability limited.
  • Price System: Listed company annual fee $12,000; pre-listed enterprise annual fee $8,000; investor portal display extra fee $5,000/year.

SME Selection Decision Matrix

Enterprise CharacteristicRecommended PlatformReasonEstimated Annual Cost
High Audit Compliance RequirementWorkivaInternal control process + version tracking$10,000-15,000
Pre-listed EnterpriseNasdaq ESGInvestor portal + financing support$8,000+
Board Governance PriorityDiligentBoard approval + data security$8,000-15,000
Data Automation NeedWorkivaAPI integration + real-time update$10,000-15,000
Budget-Limited EnterpriseDiligentSubscription price low + rapid online$8,000

2026 ESG Reporting Platform Trend Prediction

In 2026, the ESG reporting platform market will show three major trends: first, ISSB framework mainstreaming, International Sustainability Standards Board (ISSB) standard will become global unified disclosure framework, platforms will built-in ISSB full set templates; second, AI auxiliary preparation, machine learning models automatically identify ESG data missing, optimize report wording; third, supply chain ESG penetration, platforms will support supply chain questionnaire collection, achieving full chain ESG data integration. SMEs should prioritize platforms supporting ISSB framework and supply chain collection to cope with future regulatory requirements.
author: ESG Consulting Consultant
